title: Iron Man
review: Iron Man remains the gold standard for superhero origin stories, successfully launching a multi-billion dollar franchise on the back of Robert Downey Jr.'s legendary performance. The film brilliantly balances the high-stakes world of international arms dealing with a grounded redemption arc for Tony Stark. Seeing him build the Mark I in a cave with 'a box of scraps' creates a tangible sense of stakes that modern CGI-heavy films often lack. The chemistry between Tony and Pepper Potts provides a human heart to the metallic spectacle. It’s a witty, high-octane masterpiece that proved comic book movies could be sophisticated, character-driven dramas while still delivering the blockbuster action fans crave.

title: The Incredible Hulk
review: Often treated as the 'forgotten' stepchild of the MCU, The Incredible Hulk is actually a gritty and visceral monster movie that explores the tragic burden of Bruce Banner. Edward Norton brings a nervous, intellectual energy to the role, emphasizing the constant fear Banner lives in. The film moves at a brisk pace, culminating in a destructive final brawl in Harlem against the Abomination that feels heavy and impactful. While it lacks the humor of later Marvel entries, its more serious tone and focus on the military pursuit of a 'living weapon' make it a unique entry in the saga. It’s a fascinating look at what a darker, more traditional Marvel universe might have looked like before the franchise leaned fully into its signature lighthearted style.
